title: Clone Graph
description:
Given a graph represented as an **adjacency list** (an array of arrays, 1-indexed), return a **deep clone** of the graph with the same structure.

The input is an array where index `i` (1-based) holds an array of neighbor indices for node `i`.

The clone must be a completely new array structure — not a reference to the original.

**Example:**
```
adjList = [[2,4],[1,3],[2,4],[1,3]]
Node 1 connects to: 2, 4
Node 2 connects to: 1, 3
...
Result = [[2,4],[1,3],[2,4],[1,3]]  (a deep copy)
```
hints: Use a map to track cloned nodes and avoid infinite loops.
BFS or DFS both work — visit each node, clone it, then clone its neighbors.
The tricky part is handling cycles — the visited map prevents revisiting.
